meta.scoring_method: Composite normalised score: RPM×0.45 + OR×0.30 + TS×0.25, field-normalised to 5–95 range. Adjustments: headgear(×0.90) in fields≥14 runners; EDGE_ERODED(×0.85) where steam_drift_pct≥30% AND market_rank≤3. going_fit<40 → REJECT flag. model_score<50 → NO_OVERRIDE flag.
meta.steam_drift_note: steam_drift_pct = (baseline_dec - latest_dec)/baseline_dec × 100. Positive = shortening (steam), negative = drifting. CRITICAL CAVEAT: market-baseline.json prices for 13:30, 14:05, 15:15, 16:40, 17:20, 17:55 were SYNTHETIC (OR/field-size estimates dated 2026-06-02). EDGE_ERODED flags in those races reflect synthetic→real price revelation, NOT genuine shrewd-money steam. Exception: 14:40 Coronation Cup and 16:00 Derby baseline prices were real ante-post (RP 2026-06-02). Benvenuto Cellini (9/4 ante-post → Evs) is the ONLY confirmed genuine steam signal on the card.
meta.win_criteria: rank_gap >= +2 AND model_score >= 50 AND going_fit >= 40 AND no REJECT/EDGE_ERODED flags
meta.ew_criteria: rank_gap >= +3 AND price >= 8/1 (budget-dependent, consult Danny if uncertain)

biggest_shifts_vs_baseline: 1. Benvenuto Cellini (Derby 16:00): 9/4 ante-post → Evs = 38.5% genuine steam. Only real MARKET_STEAM_MAJOR on the card. EDGE_ERODED flag applied. Has ground concerns on soft.
2. Lord Melbourne (Northern Dancer 17:20): model rank 1/17, rank_gap +13, going_fit 78 (Nov Hcp soft 2024, 2-3 C&D on GTS). Upgraded from EW outsider to WIN candidate on rescore.

3. Both Linus-v1 picks invalidated: Illinois (14:40) model_score 12.1/NO_OVERRIDE; Christmas Day (16:00) rank_gap +0.5 below threshold. Derby WIN switches to Action (rank_gap +3.5, 13/1, cheekpieces).
4. Kinswoman (Dash 15:15) moved from synthetic 23/1 → real 4/1: EDGE_ERODED flag (synthetic-base caveat). Original bets-file EW outsider slot now price-eroded for the WIN criterion.
5. Going confirmed GTS with rain: 4 Derby runners REJECTED going_fit<40 (Ancient Egypt, Balzac, A Taste Of Glory, Poker — all good-to-firm specialists). HOLD card path confirmed correct.

runners.notes: Model and market agree she's the standout. Synthetic baseline caveat. Going neutral — not proven on soft but no disqualifying evidence.
Group 3 winner at Saint-Cloud (very soft) last autumn. Excellent GTS credentials. Market and model in agreement.

2nd in G2 May Hill (GTS). Market slightly shorter than model. Slight steam but below 30% threshold.

Raced only on soft/heavy — outstanding GTS credentials but model sub-50. Interesting if scoring improves with live soft-form signal.
0-13 since 2yo Listed win. Well held by today's rivals at Goodwood.

Market drifted vs synthetic — no steam concern. rank_gap +3.5: market materially undervalues vs model rank. Flagged 'needs to bounce back from latest Curragh' but today's GTS is optimal for her.

recommendation.note: Love Dynasty (19/1, going_fit 82) is tempting for EW but model_score 22.3 triggers NO_OVERRIDE — do not override without ≥2 corroborating signals beyond going fit.

runners.notes: World's highest-rated racehorse; 9/1 in 9 runs at Group 1. EDGE_ERODED from real ante-post. Genuine ground concern — spotlight notes 'not certain to run if it's a slog scenario'. GTS is workable but risk flag retained.
Won this race last year (GTS). Model and market well aligned.

2025 Derby/Irish Derby winner. MASSIVELY DRIFTED from 2/1 ante-post to 11/2 (Ryan Moore post-run comments at Chester tempered expectations). model sub-50. Linus v1 watch: if Linus had Lambourn as a pick, consider review.
Progressive; won Newbury Group 3 on reappearance. Market much shorter than model (low RPM vs top-tier). model sub-50.

recommendation.reason: Race dominated by Calandagan (EDGE_ERODED from ante-post steam) and Jan Brueghel (efficient pricing, rank_gap 0). Remaining runners all sub-50 model score (NO_OVERRIDE). No qualifier clears rank_gap≥+2 with model≥50 and no flags.

v1_invalidation.reason: model_score 12.1 — NO_OVERRIDE. Market correctly priced at 39/1 (pacemaker role). rank_gap 0. INVALIDATED.

recommendation.note: Lambourn's drift from 2/1 ante-post to 11/2 is the biggest story in this race. Ryan Moore post-Chester quotes indicate rust; market has repriced accordingly.

Ground is the residual risk — if going deteriorates to proper Soft by 16:00 the short price may be materially wrong.
recommendation.trifecta_note: Causeway void in trifecta box. Danny Path C confirmed — box stands as 3-effective-horses (Item, Benvenuto Cellini, Maltese Cross). Ancient Egypt REJECT (going_fit 35) would invalidate as trifecta leg if included — verify box composition with Danny.

win_candidate.rationale: Strongest WIN signal on the entire card on a rank_gap basis. No flags. rank_gap +13. UPGRADE from bets-file EW outsider to WIN candidate. Note: 'tempting mark if he can stage a revival; poor three starts in 2026 at 1m2f' — key risk is 2026 form dip at shorter trips; today 1m4f on GTS should be different.

17:55_jra_tokyo_trophy.void_reason: Dance In The Storm confirmed NR (absent from RP live racecard at 07:08 and 11:26). WIN bet £1.33 @ 18.5 (35/2) voids. Kinswoman+Dance double £0.50 also at risk. Saul checklist C1 applies — no replacement WIN pick assigned (requires Steve decision). No bets to be placed in this race.
17:55_jra_tokyo_trophy.saul_exposure_note: £1.83 total at-risk exposure: WIN £1.33 + double leg £0.50. Per Saul C1: handle as REQUIRES_STEVE_REVIEW until explicit decision on replacement or acceptance of void.
17:55_jra_tokyo_trophy.runners_note: Full ranked table omitted per VOID status. Fine Interview (5/2, market_rank 1, steam 79.4% from real 16/1 baseline — EDGE_ERODED) leads market. Apollo One (model_rank 1, score 95.0, 7/1) and Sondad (2nd, 81.7, 7/1) lead model ranking. Sondad won this race last year. For completeness: if a replacement WIN is ever needed, Apollo One (rank_gap +3) and Sondad (rank_gap +2) are the top non-EDGE_ERODED model picks.

recommendation.note: Awaiting Steve decision on void acceptance or replacement. Do not regenerate racecard or bets for this race without explicit Steve instruction.

rationale: GTS with rain confirmed: going correctly triggers HOLD path.

Soft-preference horses upgraded: Lord Melbourne (Nov Hcp soft winner), Princess Child (Tipperary yielding/Curragh soft-heavy), Too Soon (Epsom heavy winner), Folk Pageant (GTS 2yo debut), Hell Yeah He Did (all 3 turf runs on soft/heavy).
